grant_title: Virginia and Other States Gain Access to $50 Billion for Rural Health Transformation Fund
geographic_restrictions: Primarily rural areas in eligible states, including Virginia, West Virginia, Mississippi, Arkansas, Kentucky, Alabama, Alaska, Maine, Montana, New Mexico, North Dakota, South Dakota, Vermont, and Wyoming. Priority given to states with high rural population density, poverty rates, and healthcare disparities.
project_area: Rural healthcare infrastructure, workforce recruitment, telehealth expansion, community health programs, and healthcare innovation research.
demographic_requirements: Focus on underserved rural populations, including those with chronic diseases, limited access to specialists, and aging residents. Priority may be given to communities with documented healthcare disparities.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include state and local governments, rural hospitals, rural health clinics, healthcare providers, nonprofit organizations, and telehealth service providers operating in designated rural areas of eligible states. Funding prioritizes states with the greatest need based on population density, poverty rates, and existing healthcare infrastructure.
